mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing required returns and driving immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55% of triggered months or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment is below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms broad-based economic expansion, increasing corporate earnings forecasts and generating positive market returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline or produces negative returns when GDP growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production indicate immediate supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins and force rapid equity deleveraging.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in 60% of contraction months or the signal reverses direction during inventory restocking phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 3.0% annually forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, raising discount rates and depressing equity valuations.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when inflation crosses the threshold or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy during commodity price shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and consumer demand, directly boosting corporate revenue projections and driving positive market performance.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows or payroll growth fails to correlate with earnings revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising housing starts and falling retail sales indicates wealth effects are decoupling from consumption, creating valuation overhangs that suppress equity returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases while RSAFS declines month-over-month
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive returns in majority of cases or loses significance when mortgage rates fall below 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable inflation confirms productive capacity expansion without price pressures, supporting higher equity multiples and positive returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% while CPIAUCSL growth stays below 2.5%
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict returns when capacity utilization exceeds 85% or produces negative returns during technology-driven productivity shocks.
